A key user requirement was to avoid whitelist-like hardcoding; the implementation was tightened so packaged guards do not override shadow-guard vetoes. Those additions were reported as fixed and green before the conversation moved to rerunning the previously failing tests.
decision.reasoning_summary: The active topic at the end is still the same evaluation/testing thread, specifically whether the previously failing test can now pass and the assistant running it. The latest user turn t11 depends directly on the immediate prior exchange t9-t10, so this is `continue`, not `switch` or `resolve`. Older turns t1-t8 are resolved implementation history and are good eviction candidates. The anti-whitelist constraint in t7 is worth preserving as a compact semantic pin because it is a durable requirement that could still matter if the user follows up on the regression design.
